description.s: Make the tabbed view composable like spatial canvas: any item can be viewed as tabbed, rendering the item itself via its default view in a non-closeable "self" tab, with attachments as additional closeable tabs.
resolves an "inner view" for the item (default view, excluding itself), renders it as a full-bleed background, shows attachments as floating windows. Works on any item.
shows attachments only — one at a time as tabs. No concept of rendering the item itself.
— copy the spatial canvas pattern (~15 lines): check :innerView override → item preferredView (excluding tabbed-view) → type preferredView → nil fallback.
— prepend a non-closeable tab for the item itself, rendered via :render-child with the resolved inner view. Tab name is the item's name. This is the default active tab.
— if active tab is self, render with inner view ID; otherwise render the attachment as before.
— broaden for_type so tabbed view is available for all items, not just workspaces.
Tab bar rendering, scrolling, close buttons, mobile tab switcher overlay, tab-lib state persistence, :render-child abstraction, attachment change watchers — all reusable as-is.

Small change. The tabbed view is ~235 lines of Hob. The modification touches ~30–40 lines. Inner view resolution is a direct copy from spatial canvas (lines 176–209 of spatial-canvas-view). The rest is plumbing.
Low — nice compositional improvement but not blocking anything.
